title: Lori Throupe
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Lori Throupe
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Lori Throupe, M.A., has served as a leadership studies faculty member at Christopher Newport for over a decade. Having previous experience in co-curricular leadership development, Lori's research and passion concerns the synthesis of the academic and practical in the realm of application. In addition to teaching, Lori serves as the Faculty Director of Academic Success. Lori enjoys engaging students of leadership and followership in thoughtful conversations of generative partnership, identity, character, and impact through classroom and conference experiences.
education_entries: MA in Communication and Leadership, Gonzaga University
BA in Communication and Philosophy, Christopher Newport University
research_interests: Leadership, Followership and these related to Storytelling, Worldviews, Identity, and Character
teaching_interests: Self, Group, and Leadership, Theory and Perspectives in Leadership, Values & Ethics in Leadership, Leadership Storytelling and Personal Narrative, Leader-Follower Roles and Group Collaboration, and Followership
